title: Offload Architecture Tour
description: A guided walkthrough of Offload, a parallel test runner written in Rust with pluggable execution providers and test frameworks.

steps.title: Offload at a Glance
steps.description: Offload is a flexible parallel test runner by Imbue AI. It fans out test execution across multiple sandboxes (local processes or remote Modal environments) with automatic retry, flaky test detection, and JUnit XML reporting. The codebase follows a layered architecture: CLI entry point, configuration, providers, frameworks, orchestrator, and reporting.

steps.title: CLI Entry Point
steps.description: main.rs is the CLI entry point with subcommands: run, collect, validate, init, and logs. lib.rs re-exports the public API.

steps.title: Configuration Layer
steps.description: The config module parses offload.toml with environment variable expansion. config.rs handles loading and validation; config/schema.rs defines the typed structs.

steps.title: Execution Providers
steps.description: Providers abstract sandbox lifecycle: prepare, create, exec, destroy, and download. Three implementations: Local (child processes), Default (custom shell commands), and Modal. The connector module handles shared process spawning.

steps.title: Test Frameworks
steps.description: Frameworks handle test discovery and command generation. Pytest uses --collect-only, Cargo uses nextest list, Vitest uses --reporter=json, and Default uses custom commands. Each defines a test_id_format for matching results to discovered IDs.

steps.title: Orchestrator: The Engine
steps.description: The heart of Offload. scheduler.rs partitions tests into batches (LPT or round-robin), pool.rs manages sandboxes, spawn.rs runs batches concurrently, and runner.rs drives the full discover-schedule-execute-collect loop with retry and fail-fast.

steps.title: Reporting & Tracing
steps.description: report/ handles JUnit XML parsing, matching results to discovered test IDs. trace.rs emits Perfetto-compatible execution timelines. bundled.rs manages extraction of embedded scripts from the binary.

steps.title: The Full Picture
steps.description: Offload's architecture flows top-down: CLI parses args, config validates the TOML, the orchestrator discovers tests via the framework, schedules batches, creates sandboxes via the provider, executes tests, collects results, and handles retries.
